pi_items.metadata_value: Farmworker, Farm, Ranch, and Aquacultural Animals
pi_items.verified_value: Farmworker, Farm, Ranch, and Aquacultural Animals (BLS SOC Code 45-2093)

pi_items.source: task.json metadata field lists 5 required logs

pi_items.verified_value: Observation, Input, Activity are valid farmOS log types

pi_items.source: task.json metadata: 80 points = 4 of 5 logs at 20 points each

pi_items.verified_value: 7.5-12.5 mg/kg (single-dose) or 7.5 mg/kg (preventive therapy) for BRD in cattle
pi_items.source: FDA-approved enrofloxacin product labels and veterinary drug databases

pi_items.verified_value: 28 days is the standard FDA-approved slaughter withdrawal time for beef cattle treated with enrofloxacin
pi_items.source: Norbrook Enroflox 100 technical data, withdrawal time charts

pi_items.metadata_value: coughing, nasal discharge, elevated respiration rate, rectal temps 103-105F
pi_items.verified_value: Classical BRD signs include fever (>104F), nasal/ocular discharge, coughing, elevated respiration, depression; polymicrobial disease involving Mannheimia haemolytica, Pasteurella multocida, Histophilus somni, Mycoplasma bovis
pi_items.source: Merck Veterinary Manual, NIH/PMC, Texas A&M College of Veterinary Medicine

pi_items.metadata_value: Tulathromycin protocol mentioned as alternative for non-responders
pi_items.verified_value: Tulathromycin is FDA-approved for BRD treatment in cattle as a single-dose injection, approved since 2005
pi_items.source: FDA approval documents, Elanco, Merck Animal Health, https://www.fda.gov/animal-veterinary/cvm-updates/fda-approves-first-generic-tulathromycin-products

pi_items.metadata_value: farmOS Field Kit app, offline mode, Android
pi_items.verified_value: farmOS Field Kit is an offline-first Android app designed for farm work outside wifi/cellular range, allows log creation and sync when back online
pi_items.source: farmOS official documentation, GitHub repository, https://farmos.org/guide/app/

pi_items.metadata_value: USDA-APHIS veterinary compliance, beef marketing withdrawal period tracking
pi_items.verified_value: 9 CFR Part 86 addresses Animal Disease Traceability with record-keeping requirements (5 years for cattle); general livestock health records required for veterinary oversight and withdrawal period compliance

privileged_info_summary: The task involves a farmworker (BLS SOC 45-2093) documenting a bovine respiratory disease (BRD) outbreak in farmOS Field Kit, an offline-first Android app. BRD is a real, economically significant polymicrobial disease of cattle. Enrofloxacin at 7.5 mg/kg (verified FDA-approved dose range 7.5-12.5 mg/kg) is used for BRD treatment with a 28-day beef withdrawal period. Tulathromycin is a verified alternative BRD treatment. The task requires creating 5 logs of correct farmOS types (Observation, Input, Activity) with proper Done/Not Done status. All clinical details (fever 103-105F, respiratory signs, coughing, nasal discharge) align with verified BRD pathophysiology and treatment protocols.
